articles.title: Jharsuguda Records 41.6°C At 11:30 AM As Heatwave Sweeps Odisha - Ommcom News
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
Bhubaneswar: Jharsuguda recorded the highest temperature in Odisha, touching a scorching 41.6°C at 11:30 am on Monday. The western Odisha town saw a sharp rise in temperature compared to 38°C recorded at the same time yesterday.
According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), several other parts of the state also witnessed intense heat. Sambalpur recorded 39°C, Hirakud 38.6°C, Balasore 38.2°C, and Bhubaneswar 36.4°C at 11:30 am.
The soaring day temperatures have caused considerable discomfort to people, especially in western and coastal Odisha districts. The IMD has warned that heatwave conditions are likely to continue in many parts of the state till May 20.

articles.title: Odisha Baked at 42.9°C | Heatwave + Storm Alert Issued in 9 Districts | Kanak News Shorts - Kanak News Odisha
articles.full_text:
Odisha is currently facing extreme weather conditions with unbearable heat and rising humidity affecting daily life across the state.
On the previous day, 11 cities recorded temperatures above 40°C, with Boudh becoming the hottest at a scorching 42.9°C.
The Regional Meteorological Centre has now issued a Yellow Warning for 9 districts including Malkangiri, Koraput, Rayagada, Gajapati, Balasore, Mayurbhanj, Keonjhar, and Sundargarh.
These regions are expected to experience thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and strong winds reaching 40 to 50 kmph.
According to the weather forecast, Odisha may witness Kalbaisakhi (pre-monsoon storm activity) for the next 7 days, with unstable weather conditions likely to continue until the 23rd of this month.
Residents are advised to stay cautious due to sudden weather changes, heat stress, and storm activity across different parts of the state.
